header.name: Alex Rivera
header.headline: Data Engineer, Analytics Infrastructure, Python, SQL

summary.heading: SUMMARY
paragraphs: Data engineer focused on building reliable analytics pipelines, reusable data models, and monitoring workflows that help product and operations teams make faster decisions.
Experienced translating noisy operational signals into clean reporting datasets, compact dashboards, and automation tools that reduce manual coordination work.

entries.title: Senior Data Engineer
entries.subtitle: Analytics Platform
bullets: Built reusable ingestion and transformation jobs that consolidated product telemetry, support events, and release metadata into shared analytics tables.
Published validation checks and downstream monitoring so partner teams could trust daily reporting without manual reconciliations.
Designed stakeholder-facing dashboards that surfaced adoption, incident volume, and operational risk trends for product and operations leads.

entries.title: Analytics Engineer
entries.subtitle: Data Products
bullets: Modeled application and workflow data into consistent reporting schemas that reduced ad hoc spreadsheet analysis.
Automated recurring metric refreshes and alerting so analysts could spend more time on investigations and less on data preparation.
Partnered with engineering and customer success teams to define stable business logic for support, usage, and reliability metrics.

entries.title: Data Systems Associate
bullets: Standardized recurring operational datasets and reporting definitions that reduced manual spreadsheet cleanup for partner teams.

entries.title: Data Engineering Fellow
entries.subtitle: Cohort Program
bullets: Built end-to-end sample pipelines and documented tradeoffs across ingestion, warehousing, validation, and dashboard delivery.

entries.title: Pulseboard
entries.subtitle: Open-source telemetry dashboard starter
entries.summary: Reusable monitoring starter built for small product teams that need compact reporting and lightweight alerting.
bullets: Included sample ingestion jobs, issue rollups, and templated warehouse models for feature and incident reporting.
Added dashboard views and alert thresholds so teams could spot adoption changes and workflow regressions quickly.
